title: Google ADK Translation, Runner, Sessions, Tools, Callbacks, and Confirmation · Guided Program Studio
summary: A public-interest product team incrementally builds auditable Google ADK adapter and conformance suite from original fixed provider, interaction, tool, approval, evaluation, and failure fixtures while retaining prior Python, HTTP, prompt, loop, security, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of auditable Google ADK adapter and conformance suite while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Map product policy, provider port, loop, tool registry, session, memory, approval, events, evals, and telemetry to ADK concepts and identify gaps.
Configure Agent, model, instruction, tools, Runner, invocation, events, and async lifecycle with explicit versions and bounded behavior.
Select and test ADK session and memory services for identity, persistence, isolation, migration, retention, and replay boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Core To Adk Map
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop agent engineering team handles case 2517 during a clean package, canary, and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to map the verified custom core into ADK agent, runner, tools, sessions, callbacks, plugins, confirmation, evaluation, and deployment boundaries; loops, concurrency, tokens, time, retries, and spend stay below explicit budgets; then replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map product policy, provider port, loop, tool registry, session, memory, approval, events, evals, and telemetry to ADK concepts and identify gaps.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Adk Confirmation Conformance
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-adk-confirmation-conformance-jrpako" that uses case 1900 to map the verified custom core into ADK agent, runner, tools, sessions, callbacks, plugins, confirmation, evaluation, and deployment boundaries. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Adk Confirmation Conformance.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Adk Confirmation Conformance into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Adk Confirmation Conformance, keep Adk Confirmation Conformance connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-adk-confirmation-conformance-jrpako
# Competency: agentpy-adk-confirmation-conformance.
# Case 1900: an accessible public-service intake assistant.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter.
def evidence_agentpy_adk_confirmation_conformance_jrpako(core_contracts: int = 10, mapped_contracts: int = 10, framework_callbacks: int = 5, confirmation_paths: int = 3, hidden_framework_effects: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_jrpako = "jrpako-okaprj-1900"
    assert evidence_variant_jrpako
    evidence_axes_jrpako = (
        "authorityjrpako",
        "interaction1900",
        "toolokaprj",
        "statejrpako1900",
        "evaluation1900jrpako",
        "recoveryokaprj1900",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_jrpako)) == 6
    if core_contracts < 6 or mapped_contracts < 0:
        return False, mapped_contracts, "framework-contract-count"
    if mapped_contracts != core_contracts:
        return False, mapped_contracts, "framework-mapping-gap"
    if framework_callbacks < 3 or confirmation_paths < 2:
        return False, framework_callbacks, "framework-control-gap"
    if hidden_framework_effects > 0:
        return False, hidden_framework_effects, "framework-hidden-effect"
    assert mapped_contracts == core_contracts and hidden_framework_effects == 0
    return True, mapped_contracts, "auditable-adk-translation"
